page: Amazon Web Services c6id.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and is supported by a 54 MB L3 cache. It includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance is equipped with 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store storage, offering high-speed local read and write capabilities. It does not include any GPU accelerators. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard workloads. The single-core stress-ng score is 1894.18, and the multi-core score is 7735.64. Memory bandwidth tests show 413.67 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 58.2 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1610.0 and a multi-core score of 6004.0. In database operations, the Passmark database score is 3953.04, and Redis SET operations reach 7.96 million operations per second. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 31.82 GB/sec, and medium (7B) LLM inference speeds are poor to weak, with prompt processing at 16.29 tokens/second.

The c6id.2xlarge offers a balanced cost-efficiency profile for compute-intensive workloads requiring fast local NVMe storage. The inclusion of local NVMe SSDs makes it suitable for applications that need temporary storage with low latency, such as batch processing, distributed data stores, and moderate database operations. It is also well-suited for static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose compute tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6004.0 and Redis SET performance of 7.96 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium-sized LLM inference. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for compute-heavy workloads requiring high-speed local temporary storage, such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and 474 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering average performance for database and web serving workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for balanced workloads.

16.0 GB DDR4 memory and 474 GB local NVMe SSD storage.

Suited for software compilation, batch processing, and local data stores.
